devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । भृग्वादयस्ते मुनयो लोकानां क्षेमदर्शिनः । गोप्तर्यसति वै नॄणां पश्यन्तः पशुसाम्यताम् ॥ १ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācabhṛgvādayaste munayo lokānāṃ kṣemadarśinaḥgoptaryasati vai nṝṇāṃ paśyantaḥ paśusāmyatām
translation:
Maitreya said:
Bhṛgu and other sages who were solicitous about the well-being of the people, saw that men are reduced to brute-level at the time of anarchy. Those sages, well versed in the Vedas, invited Sunīthā, the mother of the hero (Vena), and coronated Vena as the Lord of the Earth, even though he was not approved by ministers or people.

devanagari: वीरमातरमाहूय सुनीथां ब्रह्मवादिनः । प्रकृत्यसम्मतं वेनं अभ्यषिञ्चन् पतिं भुवः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: vīramātaramāhūya sunīthāṃ brahmavādinaḥprakṛtyasammataṃ venaṃ abhyaṣiñcan patiṃ bhuvaḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
Bhṛgu and other sages who were solicitous about the well-being of the people, saw that men are reduced to brute-level at the time of anarchy. Those sages, well versed in the Vedas, invited Sunīthā, the mother of the hero (Vena), and coronated Vena as the Lord of the Earth, even though he was not approved by ministers or people.

devanagari: श्रुत्वा नृपासनगतं वेनमत्युग्रशासनम् । निलिल्युर्दस्यवः सद्यः सर्पत्रस्ता इवाखवः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: śrutvā nṛpāsanagataṃ venamatyugraśāsanamnililyurdasyavaḥ sadyaḥ sarpatrastā ivākhavaḥ
translation: Having heard that Vena, the very high-handed iron ruler, had ascended the throne, robbers hid themselves immediately like rats afraid of a serpent.

devanagari: स आरूढनृपस्थान उन्नद्धोऽष्टविभूतिभिः । अवमेने महाभागान्स्तब्धः सम्भावितः स्वतः ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: sa ārūḍhanṛpasthāna unnaddho'ṣṭavibhūtibhiḥavamene mahābhāgānstabdhaḥ sambhāvitaḥ svataḥ
translation: After occupying the king’s place he who became proud and self-conceited, regarded himself invested with the powers of the eight protectors of the world automatically, and contemptuously treated the great sages.

devanagari: एवं मदान्ध उत्सिक्तो निरङ्कुश इव द्विपः । पर्यटन् रथमास्थाय कम्पयन् इव रोदसी ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ madāndha utsikto niraṅkuśa iva dvipaḥparyaṭan rathamāsthāya kampayan iva rodasī
translation: He, who thus became blind with pride, and haughty like an elephant uncontrolled by goads, mounted his chariot and wandered over the earth shaking heaven and earth.

devanagari: न यष्टव्यं न दातव्यं न होतव्यं द्विजाः क्वचित् । इति न्यवारयद् धर्मं भेरीघोषेण सर्वशः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: na yaṣṭavyaṃ na dātavyaṃ na hotavyaṃ dvijāḥ kvacititi nyavārayad dharmaṃ bherīghoṣeṇa sarvaśaḥ
translation: With the sound of drums, he promulgated everywhere, “Oh Dvijas, you shall never perform worship, and shall give no donations and shall not perform sacrifices”. Thus he prohibited religion everywhere.

devanagari: वेनस्यावेक्ष्य मुनयो दुर्वृत्तस्य विचेष्टितम् । विमृश्य लोकव्यसनं कृपयोचुः स्म सत्रिणः ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: venasyāvekṣya munayo durvṛttasya viceṣṭitamvimṛśya lokavyasanaṃ kṛpayocuḥ sma satriṇaḥ
translation: The sages, seeing the ill-behaved conduct of Vena, and considering the public calamity, assembled at a sacrificial session and said.

devanagari: अहो उभयतः प्राप्तं लोकस्य व्यसनं महत् । दारुणि उभयतो दीप्ते इव तस्करपालयोः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: aho ubhayataḥ prāptaṃ lokasya vyasanaṃ mahatdāruṇi ubhayato dīpte iva taskarapālayoḥ
translation: “A great danger has arisen to people from both sides:—from robbers and the king, like (that to ants who find themselves in the middle of) a piece of wood burning from both sides.

devanagari: अराजकभयादेष कृतो राजातदर्हणः । ततोऽप्यासीद्भयं त्वद्य कथं स्यात् स्वस्ति देहिनाम् ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: arājakabhayādeṣa kṛto rājātadarhaṇaḥtato'pyāsīdbhayaṃ tvadya kathaṃ syāt svasti dehinām
translation: This unworthy prince was made a king on account of the fear of anarchy. But today danger has arisen even from him. How can the welfare of beings be ensured?

devanagari: अहेरिव पयःपोषः पोषकस्याप्यनर्थभृत् । वेनः प्रकृत्यैव खलः सुनीथागर्भसम्भवः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: aheriva payaḥpoṣaḥ poṣakasyāpyanarthabhṛtvenaḥ prakṛtyaiva khalaḥ sunīthāgarbhasambhavaḥ
translation: Vena who is born from the womb of Sunīthā, is hereditarily wicked. Just as the nourishing of a snake with milk is dangerous even to the man who so feeds it, Vena who has been appointed as the protector of the subjects verily desires to destroy them. Let us (however) persuade him (to behave properly) so that his sin may not touch us.

devanagari: निरूपितः प्रजापालः स जिघांसति वै प्रजाः । तथापि सान्त्वयेमामुं नास्मान् तत्पातकं स्पृशेत् ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: nirūpitaḥ prajāpālaḥ sa jighāṃsati vai prajāḥtathāpi sāntvayemāmuṃ nāsmān tatpātakaṃ spṛśet
translation: Despite our full knowledge of Vena’s wicked conduct and sinfulness, we made him the king. If, in spite of our gentle persuasions, that unrighteous fellow does not abide by our words of advice, let us burn him by our spiritual power, although he is already burnt by the reproach of the people”. Having decided thus, the sages, concealing their anger, approached that Vena, and pacifying him with conciliatory words, spoke.

devanagari: तद् विद्वद्भिः असद्वृत्तो वेनोऽस्माभिः कृतो नृपः । सान्त्वितो यदि नो वाचं न ग्रहीष्यत्यधर्मकृत् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: tad vidvadbhiḥ asadvṛtto veno'smābhiḥ kṛto nṛpaḥsāntvito yadi no vācaṃ na grahīṣyatyadharmakṛt
translation: Despite our full knowledge of Vena’s wicked conduct and sinfulness, we made him the king. If, in spite of our gentle persuasions, that unrighteous fellow does not abide by our words of advice, let us burn him by our spiritual power, although he is already burnt by the reproach of the people”. Having decided thus, the sages, concealing their anger, approached that Vena, and pacifying him with conciliatory words, spoke.

devanagari: लोकधिक्कारसन्दग्धं दहिष्यामः स्वतेजसा । एवं अध्यवसायैनं मुनयो गूढमन्यवः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: lokadhikkārasandagdhaṃ dahiṣyāmaḥ svatejasāevaṃ adhyavasāyainaṃ munayo gūḍhamanyavaḥ
translation: Despite our full knowledge of Vena’s wicked conduct and sinfulness, we made him the king. If, in spite of our gentle persuasions, that unrighteous fellow does not abide by our words of advice, let us burn him by our spiritual power, although he is already burnt by the reproach of the people”. Having decided thus, the sages, concealing their anger, approached that Vena, and pacifying him with conciliatory words, spoke.

devanagari: मुनय ऊचुः । नृपवर्य निबोधैतद् यत्ते विज्ञापयाम भोः । आयुःश्रीबलकीर्तीनां तव तात विवर्धनम् ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: munaya ūcuḥnṛpavarya nibodhaitad yatte vijñāpayāma bhoḥāyuḥśrībalakīrtīnāṃ tava tāta vivardhanam
translation:
Sages said:
“Oh Great King! Please note that what we propose to urge for your consideration will, Oh child, increase your longevity, prosperity, power and glory.

devanagari: धर्म आचरितः पुंसां वाङ्मनःकायबुद्धिभिः । लोकान् विशोकान् वितरति अथ अनन्त्यमसङ्गिनाम् ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: dharma ācaritaḥ puṃsāṃ vāṅmanaḥkāyabuddhibhiḥlokān viśokān vitarati atha anantyamasaṅginām
translation: Righteous duty (Dharma) practised in thought, word, deed and understanding, bestows on ordinary people worlds which are free from sorrows and the eternal abode (Mokṣa) to those who perform it without any attachment (to its fruits).

devanagari: स ते मा विनशेद्वीर प्रजानां क्षेमलक्षणः । यस्मिन् विनष्टे नृपतिः ऐश्वर्यादवरोहति ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: sa te mā vinaśedvīra prajānāṃ kṣemalakṣaṇaḥyasmin vinaṣṭe nṛpatiḥ aiśvaryādavarohati
translation: Oh brave king, let not your Dharma (duty) of ensuring security and well being of the subjects, be neglected (and lost) by you. For when it is lost (disregarded), the king falls from his rulership.

devanagari: राजन् असाध्वमात्येभ्यः चोरादिभ्यः प्रजा नृपः । रक्षन् यथा बलिं गृह्णन् इह प्रेत्य च मोदते ॥ १७ ॥
english_devnagari: rājan asādhvamātyebhyaḥ corādibhyaḥ prajā nṛpaḥrakṣan yathā baliṃ gṛhṇan iha pretya ca modate
translation: Oh king, a king who protects his subjects from wicked ministers and from thieves (and other miscreants) and takes revenue (from them as prescribed in Śāstras), leads a happy life.

devanagari: यस्य राष्ट्रे पुरे चैव भगवान् यज्ञपूरुषः । इज्यते स्वेन धर्मेण जनैर्वर्णाश्रमान्वितैः ॥ १८ ॥
english_devnagari: yasya rāṣṭre pure caiva bhagavān yajñapūruṣaḥijyate svena dharmeṇa janairvarṇāśramānvitaiḥ
translation: Oh illustrious King! The Lord creator of all beings, the Soul of the universe is pleased with the king who obeys his commands, and in whose kingdom and capital city, the glorious presiding deity of the sacrifice is worshipped according to their dharma, by persons who follow the duties of their respective Varṇa (social class) and āśrama (stage of life).

devanagari: तस्य राज्ञो महाभाग भगवान् भूतभावनः । परितुष्यति विश्वात्मा तिष्ठतो निजशासने ॥ १९ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya rājño mahābhāga bhagavān bhūtabhāvanaḥparituṣyati viśvātmā tiṣṭhato nijaśāsane
translation:
Oh illustrious King! The Lord creator of all beings, the Soul of the universe is pleased with the king who obeys his commands, and in whose kingdom and capital city, the glorious presiding deity of the sacrifice is worshipped according to their dharma, by persons who follow the duties of their respective Varṇa (social class) and āśrama (stage of life).
20 What is unattainable, when the Lord of the rulers of the worlds (e.g. god Brahmā etc.) is pleased? For all the worlds along with their protecting deities devoutly offer worship to him.

devanagari: तस्मिन् तुष्टे किमप्राप्यं जगतां ईश्वरेश्वरे । लोकाः सपाला ह्येतस्मै हरन्ति बलिमादृताः ॥ २० ॥
english_devnagari: tasmin tuṣṭe kimaprāpyaṃ jagatāṃ īśvareśvarelokāḥ sapālā hyetasmai haranti balimādṛtāḥ

devanagari: तं सर्वलोकामरयज्ञसङ्ग्रहं । त्रयीमयं द्रव्यमयं तपोमयम् । यज्ञैर्विचित्रैर्यजतो भवाय ते । राजन् स्वदेशान् अनुरोद्धुमर्हसि ॥ २१ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ sarvalokāmarayajñasaṅgrahaṃtrayīmayaṃ dravyamayaṃ tapomayamyajñairvicitrairyajato bhavāya terājan svadeśān anuroddhumarhasi
translation: [1] Oh King! It behoves you to approve of, and be conciliatory to the inhabitants of your country[2] who, for your prosperity, worship, by performance of various sacrifices, the Lord who is the Controller of all worlds, gods and sacrifices—the Lord who is the embodiment of three Vedas and of all sacrificial materials, and who is the object of penance.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Bhāgavata Candrikā: The Lord controls all worlds, their presiding deities and sacrifices which lead to the attainment of godhood. He is the embodiment of three Vedas which explain what is and is not the righteous duty (dharma). The Vedas prescribe both the ways of sacrifices and of penance. It is for the sake of your Liberation or prosperity that the inhabitants of your country perform different kinds of sacrifices. You should therefore follow their example.
Siddhāntapradīpa: You should direct the inhabitants of your country to perform their respective duties—the inhabitants who according to their abilities of the pravṛtti and nivṛtti type, worship the Lord who protects all the worlds, gods and sacrifices, and who is the embodiment of three Vedas which give the knowledge of sacrifice, and who is the sacrificial materials and the penance, the knowledge leading to Liberation. It is thus that you will get prosperity here and Liberation hereafter.

Footnote [2]:
svadeśān: (i) The persons whom you command—the sacrificers. Bhāgavata Candrikā (ii) The Brāhmaṇas on whom donations or gifts are conferred.—Padaratnāvalī

devanagari: यज्ञेन युष्मद्विषये द्विजातिभिः । वितायमानेन सुराः कला हरेः । स्विष्टाः सुतुष्टाः प्रदिशन्ति वाञ्छितं । तद्धेलनं नार्हसि वीर चेष्टितुम्...
english_devnagari: yajñena yuṣmadviṣaye dvijātibhiḥvitāyamānena surāḥ kalā hareḥsviṣṭāḥ sutuṣṭāḥ pradiśanti vāñchitaṃtaddhelanaṃ nārhasi vīra ceṣṭitum
translation: Oh brave king, if gods who are parts (aṃśas) of Hari, are properly worshipped and well-propitiated by performance of sacrificial sessions by the twice-born Brāhmaṇas, Kṣattriyas and Vaiśyas, they will confer whatever is desired by you. It is not, therefore, proper to disrespect them (by prohibiting sacrificial and religious performances).

devanagari: वेन उवाच । बालिशा बत यूयं वा अधर्मे धर्ममानिनः । ये वृत्तिदं पतिं हित्वा जारं पतिमुपासते ॥ २३ ॥
english_devnagari: vena uvācabāliśā bata yūyaṃ vā adharme dharmamāninaḥye vṛttidaṃ patiṃ hitvā jāraṃ patimupāsate
translation:
Vena said:
Verily, all of you are foolish. You regard nonrighteousness ṭadharma) as righteousness (dharma). What a pity! Abandoning the husband who gives you means of subsistence, you wait upon a paramour.

devanagari: अवजानन्त्यमी मूढा नृपरूपिणमीश्वरम् । नानुविन्दन्ति ते भद्रं इह लोके परत्र च ॥ २४ ॥
english_devnagari: avajānantyamī mūḍhā nṛparūpiṇamīśvaramnānuvindanti te bhadraṃ iha loke paratra ca
translation: Those fools who disregard the supreme Lord in the form of a king, do not obtain any good either here or hereafter.

devanagari: को यज्ञपुरुषो नाम यत्र वो भक्तिरीदृशी । भर्तृस्नेहविदूराणां यथा जारे कुयोषिताम् ॥ २५ ॥
english_devnagari: ko yajñapuruṣo nāma yatra vo bhaktirīdṛśībhartṛsnehavidūrāṇāṃ yathā jāre kuyoṣitām
translation: Who is this Yajñapuruṣa (the presiding deity of sacrifices) for whom you cherish such (intense) devotion like characterless women who, being absolutely devoid of love for their husbands, cherish it for their gallants.

devanagari: विष्णुर्विरिञ्चो गिरिश इन्द्रो वायुर्यमो रविः । पर्जन्यो धनदः सोमः क्षितिरग्निरपाम्पतिः ॥ २६ ॥
english_devnagari: viṣṇurviriñco giriśa indro vāyuryamo raviḥparjanyo dhanadaḥ somaḥ kṣitiragnirapāmpatiḥ
translation: Gods like Viṣṇu (the protector of the universe), Brahmā (the creator of the world), Śiva (the destroyer of the universe), Indra, Vāyu (Wind), Yama (god of death), the Sun (-god), the rain-god, the god of Wealth (Kubera), the Moon (-god), the (presiding deity of the) Earth, the Fire (-god) and Varuṇa (the Lord of Waters)—these and other gods who are competent to confer boons or pronounce curses reside in the person of the sovereign ruler. The Monarch is thus the embodiment of all gods.

devanagari: एते चान्ये च विबुधाः प्रभवो वरशापयोः । देहे भवन्ति नृपतेः सर्वदेवमयो नृपः ॥ २७ ॥
english_devnagari: ete cānye ca vibudhāḥ prabhavo varaśāpayoḥdehe bhavanti nṛpateḥ sarvadevamayo nṛpaḥ
translation: Gods like Viṣṇu (the protector of the universe), Brahmā (the creator of the world), Śiva (the destroyer of the universe), Indra, Vāyu (Wind), Yama (god of death), the Sun (-god), the rain-god, the god of Wealth (Kubera), the Moon (-god), the (presiding deity of the) Earth, the Fire (-god) and Varuṇa (the Lord of Waters)—these and other gods who are competent to confer boons or pronounce curses reside in the person of the sovereign ruler. The Monarch is thus the embodiment of all gods.

devanagari: तस्मान्मां कर्मभिर्विप्रा यजध्वं गतमत्सराः । बलिं च मह्यं हरत मत्तोऽन्यः कोऽग्रभुक् पुमान् ॥ २८ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmānmāṃ karmabhirviprā yajadhvaṃ gatamatsarāḥbaliṃ ca mahyaṃ harata matto'nyaḥ ko'grabhuk pumān
translation: Therefore, Oh Brāhmaṇas! Being free from jealousy, you worship me with your religious rites. Give me all your offerings (taxes etc.). What other person than me, deserves to be propitiated (honoured) for it?

devanagari: मैत्रेय उवाच । इत्थं विपर्ययमतिः पापीयानुत्पथं गतः । अनुनीयमानस्तद्याच्ञां न चक्रे भ्रष्टमङ्गलः ॥ २९ ॥
english_devnagari: maitreya uvācaitthaṃ viparyayamatiḥ pāpīyānutpathaṃ gataḥanunīyamānastadyācñāṃ na cakre bhraṣṭamaṅgalaḥ
translation:
Maitreya said:
Even though so humbly entreated, the wretched sinner was of such a perverted mind and had gone so astray on the wrong path, that he did not accede to their supplications, for he had lost all auspiciousness.

devanagari: इति तेऽसत्कृतास्तेन द्विजाः पण्डितमानिना । भग्नायां भव्ययाच्ञायां तस्मै विदुर चुक्रुधुः ॥ ३० ॥
english_devnagari: iti te'satkṛtāstena dvijāḥ paṇḍitamāninābhagnāyāṃ bhavyayācñāyāṃ tasmai vidura cukrudhuḥ
translation: Oh Vidura! When he, priding himself as very learned, insulted these twice-born (Brāhmaṇas), and spurned their request made in the interest of the public good, the Brāhmaṇas got angry with him.

devanagari: हन्यतां हन्यतामेष पापः प्रकृतिदारुणः । जीवन् जगदसावाशु कुरुते भस्मसाद् ध्रुवम् ॥ ३१ ॥
english_devnagari: hanyatāṃ hanyatāmeṣa pāpaḥ prakṛtidāruṇaḥjīvan jagadasāvāśu kurute bhasmasād dhruvam
translation: “Let him be slain; let this wicked fellow of terrible nature be killed. If he lives (any longer), he will soon reduce the world to ashes.

devanagari: नायमर्हत्यसद्वृत्तो नरदेववरासनम् । योऽधियज्ञपतिं विष्णुं विनिन्दत्यनपत्रपः ॥ ३२ ॥
english_devnagari: nāyamarhatyasadvṛtto naradevavarāsanamyo'dhiyajñapatiṃ viṣṇuṃ vinindatyanapatrapaḥ
translation: This shameless fellow vilifies Viṣṇu, the Supreme Deity of Sacrifices. This man of wicked conduct does not deserve the exalted seat of a monarch.

devanagari: को वैनं परिचक्षीत वेनमेकमृतेऽशुभम् । प्राप्त ईदृशमैश्वर्यं यदनुग्रहभाजनः ॥ ३३ ॥
english_devnagari: ko vainaṃ paricakṣīta venamekamṛte'śubhamprāpta īdṛśamaiśvaryaṃ yadanugrahabhājanaḥ
translation: Or who else other than this base unlucky Vena alone, will revile Viṣṇu after attaining to such sovereignty over world through His grace.”

devanagari: इत्थं व्यवसिता हन्तुं हृषयो रूढमन्यवः । निजघ्नुर्हुङ्कृतैर्वेनं हतमच्युतनिन्दया ॥ ३४ ॥
english_devnagari: itthaṃ vyavasitā hantuṃ hṛṣayo rūḍhamanyavaḥnijaghnurhuṅkṛtairvenaṃ hatamacyutanindayā
translation: With their rage flared up, the sages who thus decided to slay him, killed by their hissing (huṃ) sound, Vena who was already dead by his vilification of god Viṣṇu (Acyuta).

devanagari: ऋषिभिः स्वाश्रमपदं गते पुत्रकलेवरम् । सुनीथा पालयामास विद्यायोगेन शोचती ॥ ३५ ॥
english_devnagari: ṛṣibhiḥ svāśramapadaṃ gate putrakalevaramsunīthā pālayāmāsa vidyāyogena śocatī
translation: When the sages departed to their hermitages, Sunīthā who was full of grief, preserved the dead body of her son by means of incantations (mantras) and medicines.

devanagari: एकदा मुनयस्ते तु सरस्वत् सलिलाप्लुताः । हुत्वाग्नीन् सत्कथाश्चक्रुः उपविष्टाः सरित्तटे ॥ ३६ ॥
english_devnagari: ekadā munayaste tu sarasvat salilāplutāḥhutvāgnīn satkathāścakruḥ upaviṣṭāḥ sarittaṭe
translation: Once upon a time, those sages, having performed ablutions in the Sarasvatī, and after offering oblations to the sacrificial fire, were sitting on the bank of the river, talking about the stories of the Lord.

devanagari: वीक्ष्योत्थितान् तदोत्पातान् आहुर्लोक भयङ्करान् । अप्यभद्रमनाथाया दस्युभ्यो न भवेद्भुवः ॥ ३७ ॥
english_devnagari: vīkṣyotthitān tadotpātān āhurloka bhayaṅkarānapyabhadramanāthāyā dasyubhyo na bhavedbhuvaḥ
translation: Observing the appearance of terrible ill omens striking terror into the world, they (wishfully) said: “May no evil befall the protectionless earth from robbers”.

devanagari: एवं मृशन्त ऋषयो धावतां सर्वतोदिशम् । पांसुः समुत्थितो भूरिः चोराणामभिलुम्पताम् ॥ ३८ ॥
english_devnagari: evaṃ mṛśanta ṛṣayo dhāvatāṃ sarvatodiśampāṃsuḥ samutthito bhūriḥ corāṇāmabhilumpatām
translation: While sages were thinking thus, there arose in all directions clouds of dust from the rushing of the thieves and miscreants who ran amuck plundering (wherever they went).

devanagari: तदुपद्रवमाज्ञाय लोकस्य वसु लुम्पताम् । भर्तर्युपरते तस्मिन् अन्योन्यं च जिघांसताम् ॥ ३९ ॥
english_devnagari: tadupadravamājñāya lokasya vasu lumpatāmbhartaryuparate tasmin anyonyaṃ ca jighāṃsatām
translation: They came to know that the serious trouble to the world was caused by those free booters who plundered the wealth of the people, and by those who tried to kill each other, as the king (Vena) was dead.

devanagari: चोरप्रायं जनपदं हीनसत्त्वमराजकम् । लोकान्नावारयञ्छक्ता अपि तद्दोषदर्शिनः ॥ ४० ॥
english_devnagari: coraprāyaṃ janapadaṃ hīnasattvamarājakamlokānnāvārayañchaktā api taddoṣadarśinaḥ
translation: When anarchy prevails, the state becomes weak and the country is infested with robbers. Though they (sages) were powerful enough to control, they did not check the people as they saw the evil (involved therein).

devanagari: ब्राह्मणः समदृक् शान्तो दीनानां समुपेक्षकः । स्रवते ब्रह्म तस्यापि भिन्नभाण्डात्पयो यथा ॥ ४१ ॥
english_devnagari: brāhmaṇaḥ samadṛk śānto dīnānāṃ samupekṣakaḥsravate brahma tasyāpi bhinnabhāṇḍātpayo yathā
translation: “Even if a Brāhmaṇa who should view all beings equally and should be tranquil, neglects (to protect) the afflicted, his penance leaks away like water filled in a pot with a hole.

devanagari: नाङ्गस्य वंशो राजर्षेः एष संस्थातुमर्हति । अमोघवीर्या हि नृपा वंशेऽस्मिन् केशवाश्रयाः ॥ ४२ ॥
english_devnagari: nāṅgasya vaṃśo rājarṣeḥ eṣa saṃsthātumarhatiamoghavīryā hi nṛpā vaṃśe'smin keśavāśrayāḥ
translation: The race of the royal sage Aṅga should not come to an end, as it is in this family that there were many kings of unfailing prowess, yet completely dependent on Keśava (Viṣṇu)”.

devanagari: विनिश्चित्यैवमृषयो विपन्नस्य महीपतेः । ममन्थुरूरुं तरसा तत्रासीद्‍बाहुको नरः ॥ ४३ ॥
english_devnagari: viniścityaivamṛṣayo vipannasya mahīpateḥmamanthurūruṃ tarasā tatrāsīd‍bāhuko naraḥ
translation: Having resolved thus, the sages churned forcibly the thigh of the deceased king. Therefrom appeared a dwarfish man.

devanagari: काककृष्णोऽतिह्रस्वाङ्गो ह्रस्वबाहुर्महाहनुः । ह्रस्वपान् निम्ननासाग्रो रक्ताक्षस्ताम्रमूर्धजः ॥ ४४ ॥
english_devnagari: kākakṛṣṇo'tihrasvāṅgo hrasvabāhurmahāhanuḥhrasvapān nimnanāsāgro raktākṣastāmramūrdhajaḥ
translation: He was black as crow, had very short limbs and small arms, protruding chin, short legs, flat nose and blood- red eyes and red hair.

devanagari: तं तु तेऽवनतं दीनं किं करोमीति वादिनम् । निषीदेत्यब्रुवंस्तात स निषादस्ततोऽभवत् ॥ ४५ ॥
english_devnagari: taṃ tu te'vanataṃ dīnaṃ kiṃ karomīti vādinamniṣīdetyabruvaṃstāta sa niṣādastato'bhavat
translation: To him who bowed to them, and meekly asked them, “What I should do”, they asked him to sit down (niṣīda). Therefore, Oh Vidura, he became niṣāda (by tribe).

devanagari: तस्य वंश्यास्तु नैषादा गिरिकाननगोचराः । येनाहरत् जायमानो वेनकल्मषमुल्बणम् ॥ ४६ ॥
english_devnagari: tasya vaṃśyāstu naiṣādā girikānanagocarāḥyenāharat jāyamāno venakalmaṣamulbaṇam
translation:
As soon as he was born, he took upon himself the heinous sins of Vena. His descendants the naiṣādas took refuge in mountains, jungles, etc.
[back to top]
